pages.raw_text:
Agents of the Northern Pacific
Railroad company were before the
city council last night to make more
yelled threats about what may happen
in the event the council dares to push
the proceedings for opening Browne
street across the tracks.


The railroad crowd went before the
finance committee Monday afternoon
with bluff about changing the grade
Of the tracks, but the speakers were
finally obliged to admit that there
was nothing definite about the mat
ter" and that the railroad company
could not be bound to the plans. At
the same time the counsel for the
railroad company declared the city
must not do anything which would in
terfere with the plans.


The same sort of a bluff was re-
peated at the council meeting last
night and a species of intimidation
came up through the covert threats
Of Attorney Bum that citizens stood
ready to come before the council and
say why the councilmen should look
upon the plans as actually adopted
by the company.


AS a matter of fact, the attempted
intimidation was one of those railroad
bluffs which the companies have
worked on Spokane for years. It was
not more nor less than a threat to
have citizens who have been or will
be purchased by the railroad COM
pany to threaten the councilmen in
